recap: Like terms share the same variable(s) and power — add or subtract their coefficients to simplify.

steps: Group a squared terms: 6a squared - 2a squared = 4a squared.
Multiply x terms: x squared x x = x cubed.
Multiply y terms: y x y = y squared.

Q5.question: Simplify fully: (12p cubed q squared) / (4p squared q).

steps: Divide p terms: p cubed / p squared = p to the power 1 = p.
Divide q terms: q squared / q = q to the power 1 = q.

summary: Collect like terms by adding or subtracting their coefficients — keep the variable and power the same.
x squared and x are NOT like terms and cannot be simplified together.
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the indices.
When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the indices.
